tensions.text: GVR's own regional table only states North America's 38.0% share; it never publishes a Europe percentage, so tamBase's 'Europe ≈ 30%' figure used in the scope-down chain is an unsourced analyst estimate layered onto GVR data, not a value GVR itself reports — the 'ghost' this search group set out to find remains ungrounded.

GVR states global rack-PDU CAGR of 9.7% (2026–2033) for the market as a whole (mixing intelligent and non-intelligent, with non-intelligent holding the largest 2025 share), which sits below the 10–12% 'intelligent-PDU' growth rate the tamBase derivation attributes to IndexBox — the two growth figures describe different scopes and are not directly reconcilable from what's public.

The Europe PDUs+PSUs cross-check figure is confirmed again here at $2.45B (2024)/21.23% CAGR (R&M) and BIS Research's identical listing — reinforcing tamBase's own refinement-cycle conclusion that this combined-category ceiling is broader than rack-PDU alone and should not set the band top, consistent with the €360M cap already applied.

absences: No Grand View Research, Omdia, or any other source publishes an explicit Europe revenue-share percentage or dollar figure for the global rack-PDU market — only North America's 38% share is disclosed, leaving Europe's share entirely inferred.

Omdia's Rack PDU Annual Market Analysis – 2025 and Rack PDU Market Tracker – 2025 (which include vendor shares and forecasts to 2030) are subscription-gated; no regional or Europe-specific breakdown was retrievable from public previews.

The IndexBox EU/world/country-level Rack PDUs reports (78-report cluster including Germany and UK pages) exist for purchase but their headline market-size figures are entirely paywalled — no country or regional euro/dollar figures were surfaced.
No Central Europe (CE-7) specific rack-PDU market figure or share was found in any source; the CE-7 scope-down remains unvalidated by any directly-scoped dataset.

No source decomposes the rack-PDU market by intelligent vs. non-intelligent revenue split by region, which would be needed to validate the 10–12% intelligent-PDU growth figure cited in tamBase's derivation against a primary table.
